content.problemStatement:
Write a program to read a number of more than one digit, reverse the number and display the sum of digits of numbers.


Input Format
Number should have more than one digit.

code.Python3:
# Read input
number = int(input())

reversed_number = 0
sum_of_digits = 0

# Check if the number is negative
is_negative = False
if number < 0:
    is_negative = True
    number = -number  # Make the number positive for reversal and summing

original_number = number

while number != 0:
    digit = number % 10        # Extract the last digit
    sum_of_digits += digit     # Add it to the sum of digits
    reversed_number = reversed_number * 10 + digit  # Build the reversed number
    number //= 10              # Remove the last digit

# If the original number was negative, make the reversed number negative
if is_negative:
    reversed_number = -reversed_number

# Display the reversed number and the sum of digits
print("Reversed:", reversed_number)
print("Sum:", sum_of_digits)
